{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Secretary",
  "description": "Berit Global Limited is an indigenous Company and dully registered under the Corporate Affairs Commission of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

Berit Global Limited is a wholly owned Nigerian company with a team of highly dedicated and professional staff skilled in providing specialized and integrated services to the Oil and Gas sector, Power sector, Energy sector, FMCGs, Industrial Engineering Services and Building Services.

Our proven and quality service delivery is second to none in the areas of Front End Engineering Design (FEED), Detailed Engineering Design (DED), Basic Engineering Design (BED), Drafting and As-Built Design, Procurement and Supply, Construction, Project Management, Manpower Supply, Installation, Commissioning, Decommissioning, Maintenance and Sales.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Secretary

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	We are looking for an experienced secretary to supervise daily support operations of our company and plan the most efficient administrative procedures. You will lead a team of professionals to complete a range of administrative duties in different departments.
	A great administration manager has excellent communication and organizational skills.
	The ideal candidate will be well-versed in departmental procedures and policies and will be able to actively discover new ways to do the job more efficiently.


Salary
N150,000 - N300,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidate should send there CV to:&nbsp;beritgloballimited.ng@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position&nbsp; as the subject of the mail.",

Secretary Job at Berit Global Limited




        
    

        
        Berit Global Limited is an indigenous Company and dully registered under the Corporate Affairs Commission of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

Berit Global Limited is a wholly owned Nigerian company with a team of highly dedicated and professional staff skilled in providing specialized and integrated services to the Oil and Gas sector, Power sector, Energy sector, FMCGs, Industrial Engineering Services and Building Services.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our proven and quality service delivery is second to none in the areas of Front End Engineering Design (FEED), Detailed Engineering Design (DED), Basic Engineering Design (BED), Drafting and As-Built Design, Procurement and Supply, Construction, Project Management, Manpower Supply, Installation, Commissioning, Decommissioning, Maintenance and Sales.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Secretary

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 


	We are looking for an experienced secretary to supervise daily support operations of our company and plan the most efficient administrative procedures. You will lead a team of professionals to complete a range of administrative duties in different departments.
	A great administration manager has excellent communication and organizational skills.
	The ideal candidate will be well-versed in departmental procedures and policies and will be able to actively discover new ways to do the job more efficiently.


Salary
N150,000 - N300,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send there CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Auditor",
  "description": "Dios Dlite is an innovative indigenous manufacturer of guilt-free products in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor

Job Location:&nbsp;Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Brief


	The Internal Auditor will support Dios Dlite in strengthening internal controls, improving operational efficiency, and ensuring compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	The role involves reviewing financial and operational processes, identifying risks, and providing practical recommendations to enhance governance and accountability.


Job Responsibilities
Audit &amp; Risk Management:


	Conduct internal audits covering financial, operational, and process controls
	Review compliance with internal policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements
	Identify control weaknesses, risks, and inefficiencies and recommend corrective actions
	Support the development and implementation of risk mitigation measures


Financial &amp; Operational Review:


	Review expenses, payments, and inventory-related processes for accuracy and compliance
	Verify supporting documentation for transactions and expenditures
	Assist in monitoring asset usage and safeguarding company resources


Reporting &amp; Documentation:


	Prepare clear audit working papers, reports, and findings
	Communicate audit observations and recommendations to management
	Follow up on audit issues to ensure timely implementation of agreed actions


Process Improvement &amp; Advisory:


	Support management with process reviews and improvement initiatives
	Provide advisory support on internal controls and best practices
	Promote a culture of accountability, transparency, and compliance


Requirements &amp; Qualifications
Education &amp; Professional Qualification:


	B.Sc or HND in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline
	Professional qualification (ICAN, ACCA, or in progress) is an added advantage


Experience:


	Minimum of 2&ndash;3 years of experience in internal audit, external audit, accounting, or control-related roles
	Experience in FMCG, manufacturing, food processing, or retail environments is an advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong understanding of internal control systems and audit processes
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills
	High attention to detail and accuracy
	Strong report-writing and documentation skills
	Integrity, confidentiality, and professional judgment
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic accounting systems.


Benefits


	Salary: N150,000 - N180,000 per month.
	Pension
	13th Month Salary
	Leave Allowance
	Performance Bonus
	Product Discount
	HMO.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Interested and qulified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;primofinessejobs@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only selected candidates will be contacted.",

Internal Auditor Job at Dios Dlite




        
    

        
        Dios Dlite is an innovative indigenous manufacturer of guilt-free products in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor

Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Brief
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Internal Auditor will support Dios Dlite in strengthening internal controls, improving operational efficiency, and ensuring compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	The role involves reviewing financial and operational processes, identifying risks, and providing practical recommendations to enhance governance and accountability.


Job Responsibilities
Audit & Risk Management:


	Conduct internal audits covering financial, operational, and process controls
	Review compliance with internal policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements
	Identify control weaknesses, risks, and inefficiencies and recommend corrective actions
	Support the development and implementation of risk mitigation measures


Financial & Operational Review:


	Review expenses, payments, and inventory-related processes for accuracy and compliance
	Verify supporting documentation for transactions and expenditures
	Assist in monitoring asset usage and safeguarding company resources


Reporting & Documentation:


	Prepare clear audit working papers, reports, and findings
	Communicate audit observations and recommendations to management
	Follow up on audit issues to ensure timely implementation of agreed actions


Process Improvement & Advisory:


	Support management with process reviews and improvement initiatives
	Provide advisory support on internal controls and best practices
	Promote a culture of accountability, transparency, and compliance


Requirements & Qualifications
Education & Professional Qualification:


	B.Sc or HND in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline
	Professional qualification (ICAN, ACCA, or in progress) is an added advantage


Experience:


	Minimum of 2–3 years of experience in internal audit, external audit, accounting, or control-related roles
	Experience in FMCG, manufacturing, food processing, or retail environments is an advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong understanding of internal control systems and audit processes
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills
	High attention to detail and accuracy
	Strong report-writing and documentation skills
	Integrity, confidentiality, and professional judgment
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and basic accounting systems.


Benefits


	Salary: N150,000 - N180,000 per month.
	Pension
	13th Month Salary
	Leave Allowance
	Performance Bonus
	Product Discount
	HMO.


Method of Application 

Interested and qulified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only selected candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Support Officer",
  "description": "Reposebay Human Resource Limited offers HR consulting and career development services through a robust technology web application that is powered by Artificial intelligence (AI). This solution has helped prepare young graduates for the world of work and has helped MSMEs afford professional HR consultants to provide services in workforce management, employee engagement, retention, standard operating procedures, and workplace policies.&nbsp;Our all-inclusive solution has also helped job seekers get the right employment and business owners get the best out of their employees.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Support Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos (Ikoyi / Other Branches as required)
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Information Technology
Reports To: General Manager / Operations Manager

Job Summary


	The IT Support Officer is responsible for maintaining the company&rsquo;s IT systems, networks, POS systems, and hardware infrastructure to ensure smooth daily operations across theSupamart branches.
	The role involves troubleshooting technical issues, maintaining system security, supporting staff with technology needs, and ensuring the reliability of all digital systems used within the supermarket operations.


Key Responsibilities
Technical Support:


	Provide technical support to staff on computers, POS systems, printers, scanners, and network devices.
	Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, and connectivity issues.
	Respond promptly to IT-related support requests from branches.


POS &amp; Retail System Management:


	Maintain and troubleshoot Point of Sale (POS) machines and software.
	Ensure seamless billing and transaction processing.
	Support the integration between POS systems and inventory management software.


Network &amp; Internet Management:


	Manage the company&rsquo;s internet connectivity and local network infrastructure.
	Monitor Wi-Fi, routers, switches, and network performance.
	Ensure stable connectivity across all branches.


Hardware Maintenance:


	Maintain computers, printers, barcode scanners, CCTV systems, and other IT equipment.
	Perform regular maintenance and updates of hardware devices.
	Coordinate repair or replacement of faulty equipment.


System Installation &amp; Updates:


	Install and configure software, operating systems, and business applications.
	Ensure systems are regularly updated and protected from vulnerabilities.


Data Security &amp; Backup:


	Implement and monitor data backup procedures.
	Ensure confidential company data and financial records are properly secured.
	Maintain antivirus and cybersecurity measures.


IT Asset Management:


	Maintain an updated inventory of IT assets and equipment.
	Track assignment of devices to staff and branches.


Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Maintain records of IT issues, maintenance activities, and resolutions.
	Prepare periodic IT support reports for management.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Diploma in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field.


Experience


	1&ndash;3 years of experience in IT support, network administration, or retail system support.


Technical Skills:


	Knowledge of POS systems and retail technology.
	Understanding of network troubleshooting (routers, switches, LAN/WAN).
	Experience with Windows operating systems and software installation.
	Familiarity with basic cybersecurity and data backup procedures.


Soft Skills:


	Strong problem-solving ability
	Good communication skills
	Ability to work under pressure
	Attention to detail
	Ability to support multiple locations when required


Work Conditions:


	Work schedule may include visits to different branches when required.
	Occasional after-hours support may be required during system failures or upgrades.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;franklin@reposebayhr.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",

IT Support Officer Job at Reposebay Human Resource Limited




        
    

        
        Reposebay Human Resource Limited offers HR consulting and career development services through a robust technology web application that is powered by Artificial intelligence (AI). This solution has helped prepare young graduates for the world of work and has helped MSMEs afford professional HR consultants to provide services in workforce management, employee engagement, retention, standard operating procedures, and workplace policies. Our all-inclusive solution has also helped job seekers get the right employment and business owners get the best out of their employees.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Support Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos (Ikoyi / Other Branches as required)
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Information Technology
Reports To: General Manager / Operations Manager

Job Summary


	The IT Support Officer is responsible for maintaining the company’s IT systems, networks, POS systems, and hardware infrastructure to ensure smooth daily operations across theSupamart branches.
	The role involves troubleshooting technical issues, maintaining system security, supporting staff with technology needs, and ensuring the reliability of all digital systems used within the supermarket operations.


Key Responsibilities
Technical Support:


	Provide technical support to staff on computers, POS systems, printers, scanners, and network devices.
	Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, and connectivity issues.
	Respond promptly to IT-related support requests from branches.


POS & Retail System Management:


	Maintain and troubleshoot Point of Sale (POS) machines and software.
	Ensure seamless billing and transaction processing.
	Support the integration between POS systems and inventory management software.


Network & Internet Management:


	Manage the company’s internet connectivity and local network infrastructure.
	Monitor Wi-Fi, routers, switches, and network performance.
	Ensure stable connectivity across all branches.


Hardware Maintenance:


	Maintain computers, printers, barcode scanners, CCTV systems, and other IT equipment.
	Perform regular maintenance and updates of hardware devices.
	Coordinate repair or replacement of faulty equipment.


System Installation & Updates:


	Install and configure software, operating systems, and business applications.
	Ensure systems are regularly updated and protected from vulnerabilities.


Data Security & Backup:


	Implement and monitor data backup procedures.
	Ensure confidential company data and financial records are properly secured.
	Maintain antivirus and cybersecurity measures.


IT Asset Management:


	Maintain an updated inventory of IT assets and equipment.
	Track assignment of devices to staff and branches.


Documentation & Reporting:


	Maintain records of IT issues, maintenance activities, and resolutions.
	Prepare periodic IT support reports for management.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor's Degree or Diploma in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field.


Experience


	1–3 years of experience in IT support, network administration, or retail system support.


Technical Skills:


	Knowledge of POS systems and retail technology.
	Understanding of network troubleshooting (routers, switches, LAN/WAN).
	Experience with Windows operating systems and software installation.
	Familiarity with basic cybersecurity and data backup procedures.


Soft Skills:


	Strong problem-solving ability
	Good communication skills
	Ability to work under pressure
	Attention to detail
	Ability to support multiple locations when required


Work Conditions:


	Work schedule may include visits to different branches when required.
	Occasional after-hours support may be required during system failures or upgrades.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mechanical / Electrical Engineer",
  "description": "At Ucalux, we distinguish ourselves in the construction industry through its unparalleled offerings, backed by over 50 years of combined international and local construction expertise. Our track record includes successfully delivering numerous iconic projects within the Lagos skyline and beyond.

Our management team is strategically positioned to fulfill our clients&rsquo; requirements by leveraging an extensive network of specialists and suppliers across the entire construction sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical / Electrical Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The M&amp;E Engineer oversees the design, installation, and optimization of mechanical (HVAC, plumbing, fire suppression) and electrical (power distribution, lighting, controls) infrastructure.
	This role bridges the gap between hardware and electronic control to ensure operational efficiency, safety compliance, and system reliability.


Key Responsibilities


	System Design &amp; Documentation: Develop detailed 2D/3D CAD models, electrical schematics, and piping layouts for integrated systems.
	Installation Oversight: Coordinate with contractors to supervise the installation of HVAC, power distribution units, and industrial machinery.
	Troubleshooting &amp; Repair: Diagnose complex malfunctions involving mechanical, electrical, and pneumatic components to minimize downtime.
	Project Management: Manage project lifecycles, including budgeting, resource allocation, and meeting strict delivery timelines.
	Compliance &amp; Safety: Enforce industry standards (e.g., NEC, NFPA, ASHRAE) and conduct rigorous safety risk assessments.
	Performance Optimization: Identify and implement energy-efficient technologies or automation to reduce operational costs.


Job Requirements


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering (Master&#39;s preferred).
	Experience: 2-5 years in M&amp;E system design, construction or facility management.
	Certifications: Professional Engineer (PE) license or industry-specific certifications (e.g., CEM for Energy Management) is an added advantage.
	Location: Based at the project sites.


Technical Proficiency:


	Software: Expert-level knowledge of AutoCAD, SolidWorks, or Revit.
	Systems: Hands-on experience with PLCs (Siemens/Allen-Bradley), HVAC, and power distribution.
	Soft Skills: Strong analytical problem-solving, project management, and the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.


Benefits


	HMO
	Pension
	Performance bonus
	Other benefits.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to:&nbsp;olabisi.akintola@ucaluxng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-20",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

  },

    }
  }
}



Mechanical / Electrical Engineer Job at Ucalux




        
    

        
        At Ucalux, we distinguish ourselves in the construction industry through its unparalleled offerings, backed by over 50 years of combined international and local construction expertise. Our track record includes successfully delivering numerous iconic projects within the Lagos skyline and beyond.

Our management team is strategically positioned to fulfill our clients’ requirements by leveraging an extensive network of specialists and suppliers across the entire construction sector.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical / Electrical Engineer

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The M&E Engineer oversees the design, installation, and optimization of mechanical (HVAC, plumbing, fire suppression) and electrical (power distribution, lighting, controls) infrastructure.
	This role bridges the gap between hardware and electronic control to ensure operational efficiency, safety compliance, and system reliability.


Key Responsibilities


	System Design & Documentation: Develop detailed 2D/3D CAD models, electrical schematics, and piping layouts for integrated systems.
	Installation Oversight: Coordinate with contractors to supervise the installation of HVAC, power distribution units, and industrial machinery.
	Troubleshooting & Repair: Diagnose complex malfunctions involving mechanical, electrical, and pneumatic components to minimize downtime.
	Project Management: Manage project lifecycles, including budgeting, resource allocation, and meeting strict delivery timelines.
	Compliance & Safety: Enforce industry standards (e.g., NEC, NFPA, ASHRAE) and conduct rigorous safety risk assessments.
	Performance Optimization: Identify and implement energy-efficient technologies or automation to reduce operational costs.


Job Requirements


	Education: Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering (Master's preferred).
	Experience: 2-5 years in M&E system design, construction or facility management.
	Certifications: Professional Engineer (PE) license or industry-specific certifications (e.g., CEM for Energy Management) is an added advantage.
	Location: Based at the project sites.


Technical Proficiency:


	Software: Expert-level knowledge of AutoCAD, SolidWorks, or Revit.
	Systems: Hands-on experience with PLCs (Siemens/Allen-Bradley), HVAC, and power distribution.
	Soft Skills: Strong analytical problem-solving, project management, and the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.


Benefits


	HMO
	Pension
	Performance bonus
	Other benefits.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
